prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response:
Being newly retained by the High Steward of Revenue to chronicle the affairs of the House of Software, I set quill to parchment for the third quarter, which is, puzzlingly, neither a harvest nor a moon.  
Their wares reside in a mystic Cloud—no keep nor granary—yet from this vaporish castle flows monthly recurring tribute, and when “churn” occurs they lament as if serfs absconded, then perform onboarding rites to tether them anew.  
The company waged battle against the dragon Burn Rate, whose fiery hunger was tamped by a Series B caravan of coin, thereby lengthening the so-called runway, a stone road I am told is for launching iron geese of finance.  
Uptime was proclaimed 99.9%, which I record as bells tolling almost without cease, while Net Promoter Saints number 52 and the sales pipeline grows fatter than a Michaelmas boar, yet all beseeched me to mind the funnel lest qualified leads seep away like ale from a cracked tun.
